page.content: In the second quarter of fiscal year 2025, Deere & Company reported crop protection product sales categorized into herbicides, insecticides, and fungicides, with total units sold reaching 10,000 and total revenue amounting to ¥500M. Specifically, the herbicide segment sold 5,000 units, generating revenue of ¥250M, and holding a market share of 30%. The sales performance in this category was classified as total sales with customer feedback scoring 50% satisfaction in North Asia, where 3,000 units were sold, and 2,000 units sold in South Asia. The insecticide category sold 3,000 units, generating ¥150M, with a market share of 20%. This segment also recorded total sales performance of 30%, with 2,000 units sold in North Asia and 1,000 units in South Asia.

page.content: In the fungicide category, 2,000 units were sold, yielding revenue of ¥100M, which corresponds to a market share of 15%. The sales performance was again noted as total sales, with customer feedback reflecting 25% satisfaction, attributed to 1,500 units sold in North Asia and 500 units in South Asia. Overall, the market performance indicated a customer satisfaction rate of 85% in the overall market, coupled with a sales growth of 10%. The company maintained a total of 30 branches and an employee headcount of 200, contributing to the positive outlook in the crop protection sector.
